analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-382

POL-382.title: IMPLEMENT: Canonical doctrine, skill references, graph-native adoption flow
POL-382.body:
## Objective

Implement the [POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) adoption authority model so Polaris adoption produces correct skill references, uses Polaris-native graph/map intelligence, and generates a canonical POLARIS_RULES.md with preserved genesis instructions.

## Context

Created from ANALYZE source issue [POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris). The analysis found drift across command references, external repo-analysis defaults, POLARIS_RULES.md generation, root surfaces, and genesis migration. Current skill packets still reference `npm run polaris --`, package metadata exposes `polaris-cli` while help says `polaris`, and repo-analysis defaults still prefer GitNexus despite Polaris now having its own graph command surface.

Analysis artifact: smartdocs/raw/[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris)-canonical-doctrine-genesis-analysis.md

## Goal

Deliver an adoption flow where global Polaris commands, bundled skills, graph/map navigation, POLARIS_RULES.md, root cognition, agent adapters, and genesis migration all follow one coherent authority model.

## Scope

* package.json
* src/cli/**
* src/config/**
* src/graph/**
* src/workspace/**
* .polaris/skills/**
* POLARIS_RULES.md
* polaris.config.json
* smartdocs/raw/[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris)-canonical-doctrine-genesis-analysis.md
* docs/superpowers/**
* smartdocs/doctrine/**
* test/**
* src/cli/*.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Polaris skill packets and bundled workspace skills reference the correct global Polaris command surface.
* Repo-analysis guidance uses Polaris graph/map as the primary path, not GitNexus.
* POLARIS_RULES.md has a canonical template source and is packaged for adoption.
* Root surfaces and agent adapters consistently point at the intended authority.
* Existing instruction content is preserved with provenance during adoption.
* Fresh-repo adoption proof covers command references, graph behavior, root surfaces, and genesis migration.
* Tests pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts src/cli/adopt-workspace.test.ts src/cli/adopt-assets.test.ts src/cli/adopt-genesis.test.ts src/cli/adopt-instructions.test.ts src/cli/init.test.ts src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts test/integration/fresh-repo-adoption.test.ts
* polaris-cli graph build
* polaris-cli graph query runInit
* polaris-cli graph impact runInit

## Ordering

* Child issues execute in the order listed in .polaris/clusters/<this-issue>/clusters.json.
* Command-surface and graph-native work must complete before adoption generation children that reference those surfaces.

## Non-goals

* Do not execute polaris-run from the ANALYZE issue.
* Do not remove POLARIS.md entirely; keep it as route cognition unless a child proves a narrower compatibility behavior is required.
* Do not reintroduce GitNexus as the default repo-analysis dependency.

POL-388.title: IMPLEMENT: Wire adoption flow and fresh-repo proof for canonical rules
POL-388.body:
## Objective

Wire the corrected command, graph, rules, root surface, and genesis migration behavior into `polaris init --adopt` and prove it on a fresh repo path.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) requires the final adoption flow to generate canonical POLARIS_RULES.md, preserve genesis instruction content, install workspace assets, use Polaris graph/map guidance, and leave adopted repos immediately operable. Earlier children establish the underlying command, graph, template, root surface, and migration behavior.

## Goal

Make the end-to-end adoption flow execute phases in the correct order and verify the resulting repository surfaces.

## Scope

* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/init.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-assets.ts
* src/cli/adopt-report.ts
* src/cli/adopt-report.test.ts
* test/integration/fresh-repo-adoption.test.ts
* package.json
* src/workspace/**
* docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-workspace-asset-bundling-design.md
* smartdocs/raw/[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris)-canonical-doctrine-genesis-analysis.md

## Acceptance Criteria

* `polaris init --adopt` writes or installs `POLARIS_RULES.md` before agent pointer reconciliation depends on it.
* Adoption phase order is deterministic and documented in tests.
* Fresh-repo adoption proof verifies `POLARIS_RULES.md`, root `POLARIS.md`, root `SUMMARY.md`, agent adapters, workspace skills/roles, SmartDocs scaffold, graph result reporting, and genesis provenance.
* Adoption reports include graph status and instruction migration outcomes.
* Runtime/staging hygiene still excludes transient artifacts.
* All targeted and full tests pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/init.test.ts src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts src/cli/adopt-assets.test.ts src/cli/adopt-report.test.ts test/integration/fresh-repo-adoption.test.ts
* npm pack --dry-run
* polaris-cli init --adopt --dry-run

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-387](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-387/implement-consolidate-genesis-instruction-migration-and-provenance) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not execute a governed implementation run from this analysis session.
* Do not change finalizer delivery semantics beyond adoption output staging hygiene.
* Do not require external repo-analysis providers for the fresh-repo proof.

POL-387.title: IMPLEMENT: Consolidate genesis instruction migration and provenance
POL-387.body:
## Objective

Unify existing agent instruction preservation into one deterministic genesis migration path with provenance.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) analysis found overlapping behavior between `adopt-instructions.ts` and `adopt-genesis.ts`. One path archives supported instruction files into `smartdocs/raw/migrated-instructions`; the other prompts for Anthropic compression into `smartdocs/doctrine/active`. The baseline adoption path should preserve content losslessly and not require a specific external model.

## Goal

Make adoption preserve original instruction content, write thin adapters, and record provenance across all supported instruction surfaces.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts
* src/cli/adopt-instructions.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-genesis.ts
* src/cli/adopt-genesis.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-report.ts
* src/cli/adopt-report.test.ts
* src/cli/adoption-inventory.ts
* src/cli/adoption-inventory.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-scan.ts
* smartdocs/raw/migrated-instructions/**
* .polaris/adoption-provenance.json
* docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-workspace-asset-bundling-design.md

## Acceptance Criteria

* One canonical migration path handles `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.cursor/rules/*.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.aider.conf.yml`, `AIDER.md`, and other already-supported instruction files.
* Original content is preserved losslessly under SmartDocs raw migrated-instructions storage.
* Thin adapters point at `POLARIS_RULES.md` after migration.
* `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json` records source path, backup path, decision, timestamp, and migration outcome.
* Optional distilled genesis doctrine is not required for baseline adoption success.
* Tests cover accepted, refused/skipped, existing pointer, unsupported path, and provenance behavior.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-instructions.test.ts src/cli/adopt-genesis.test.ts src/cli/adoption-inventory.test.ts src/cli/adopt-report.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-386](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-386/implement-normalize-adoption-root-surfaces-and-agent-pointers) being Done first so pointer targets are settled.

## Non-goals

* Do not require Anthropic, GitNexus, or any external provider for baseline migration.
* Do not promote migrated raw instruction files directly to active doctrine.
* Do not overwrite unsupported files.

POL-386.title: IMPLEMENT: Normalize adoption root surfaces and agent pointers
POL-386.body:
## Objective

Align adoption root surfaces so POLARIS_RULES.md is the global governance authority and [POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md](<http://POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md>) remain route cognition surfaces.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) analysis found the current root scaffold points agent files to POLARIS.md while other components treat POLARIS_RULES.md as canonical. Root POLARIS.md should remain useful route cognition, but agent adapters need a clear governance pointer.

## Goal

Update root surface scaffolding and instruction pointer text so adopted repos have a coherent final root layout.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-workspace.ts
* src/cli/adopt-workspace.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts
* src/cli/adopt-instructions.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* POLARIS_RULES.md
* src/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md
* docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-workspace-asset-bundling-design.md

## Acceptance Criteria

* Adopted root layout distinguishes global `POLARIS_RULES.md` from route-local `POLARIS.md` and informational `SUMMARY.md`.
* `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and other supported adapters point to `POLARIS_RULES.md` where appropriate.
* `POLARIS.md` root scaffold links to `POLARIS_RULES.md` without claiming to be the single global authority.
* Tests cover new pointer text and non-overwrite behavior.
* Symlink-safety behavior remains intact.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-workspace.test.ts src/cli/adopt-instructions.test.ts src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-385](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-385/implement-package-polaris-native-polaris-rulesmd-template-for-adoption) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement genesis archival logic in this child beyond pointer integration.
* Do not change graph build semantics.
* Do not remove existing root POLARIS.md support.

POL-385.title: IMPLEMENT: Package Polaris-native POLARIS_RULES.md template for adoption
POL-385.body:
## Objective

Make the Polaris repo's canonical POLARIS_RULES.md available as a versioned template shipped with the package and used by adoption.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) requires the Polaris-native rules file to become the reusable source template for adopted repositories. The approved workspace asset design says `src/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md` should exist, but this checkout currently has root `POLARIS_RULES.md` and no `src/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md`.

## Goal

Create a maintained template source for POLARIS_RULES.md and ensure build/adoption packaging includes it.

## Scope

* POLARIS_RULES.md
* src/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md
* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-assets.ts
* src/cli/adopt-assets.test.ts
* package.json
* docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-workspace-asset-bundling-design.md
* smartdocs/raw/[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris)-canonical-doctrine-genesis-analysis.md

## Acceptance Criteria

* A Polaris-native POLARIS_RULES.md template exists in the package workspace source.
* Build output includes the template under `dist/workspace`.
* Adoption generation can copy or patch the template rather than synthesizing unrelated rules from scratch.
* Template content includes skill routing, map navigation, graph navigation, route cognition discovery, governance expectations, canonical document locations, runtime artifacts, and evidence requirements.
* Tests verify the template is written or copied during relevant adoption phases.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts src/cli/adopt-assets.test.ts
* test -f dist/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md
* rg -n "Graph Navigation|Skill Command Routing|Runtime Boundaries" POLARIS_RULES.md src/workspace/POLARIS_RULES.md

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-384](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-384/implement-use-polaris-graph-and-map-as-the-repo-intelligence-surface) being Done first so the template uses graph-native language.

## Non-goals

* Do not finalize the entire adoption orchestration in this child.
* Do not migrate user instruction files in this child.
* Do not remove root POLARIS.md.

POL-384.title: IMPLEMENT: Use Polaris graph and map as the repo intelligence surface
POL-384.body:
## Objective

Replace GitNexus-default repo-analysis guidance with Polaris-native graph and map usage across config, skills, and docs.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) analysis and user correction established that Polaris should no longer rely on GitNexus. Polaris now has its own `graph build`, `graph query`, and `graph impact` command surface. Skills should prefer Polaris graph/map and fall back to `rg` plus direct inspection.

## Goal

Make Polaris graph/map the primary code-intelligence path for analyze/run skills and adoption guidance.

## Scope

* polaris.config.json
* src/config/**
* src/config/provider-detect.ts
* src/graph/**
* src/cli/graph.ts
* .polaris/skills/polaris-analyze/**
* .polaris/skills/polaris-run/**
* src/workspace/.polaris/skills/polaris-analyze/**
* src/workspace/.polaris/skills/polaris-run/**
* POLARIS_RULES.md
* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* smartdocs/doctrine/**
* docs/superpowers/**

## Acceptance Criteria

* Default repo-analysis configuration no longer prefers GitNexus for Polaris-managed repos.
* Analyze/run linked-skill instructions describe Polaris graph/map as primary and `rg`/direct inspection as fallback.
* Skill steps use `polaris graph query`, `polaris graph impact`, and `polaris map query` where relevant.
* Generated `POLARIS_RULES.md` includes graph navigation guidance.
* Tests or static checks verify stale GitNexus-default references are gone from active skill surfaces.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* polaris-cli graph build
* polaris-cli graph query runInit
* polaris-cli graph impact runInit
* rg -n "GitNexus|gitnexus" .polaris/skills src/workspace/.polaris/skills POLARIS_RULES.md src/cli/adopt-rules.ts polaris.config.json

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-383](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-383/implement-normalize-polaris-global-command-and-skill-references) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not delete historical doctrine solely to remove old GitNexus mentions.
* Do not require graph success as a hard blocker for all adoption flows unless the existing graph policy already requires it.
* Do not remove direct inspection fallback.

POL-383.title: IMPLEMENT: Normalize Polaris global command and skill references
POL-383.body:
## Objective

Make the Polaris package, CLI help, skill packets, bundled skills, and generated rules reference one correct global command surface.

## Context

[POL-381](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-381/analyze-canonical-doctrine-generation-and-genesis-migration-in-polaris) analysis found command drift: skills still say `npm run polaris --`, package `bin` exposes only `polaris-cli`, CLI help says `polaris`, and user-facing commands include `polaris-analyze`. This causes bootloader failure and makes adopted repos unreliable.

## Goal

Expose and document the canonical command surface, then update all Polaris skill and rules references to use it consistently.

## Scope

* package.json
* src/cli/index.ts
* src/cli/**/graph*.ts
* .polaris/skills/**
* src/workspace/.polaris/skills/**
* POLARIS_RULES.md
* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* smartdocs/doctrine/**
* docs/superpowers/**

## Acceptance Criteria

* Package metadata exposes the intended canonical global CLI name and any approved compatibility aliases.
* CLI help text matches the exposed executable name.
* Skill bootloaders use the canonical global command, not `npm run polaris --`.
* `.polaris/skills/**` and `src/workspace/.polaris/skills/**` contain no stale `npm run polaris --` references except in historical docs explicitly marked as historical.
* `POLARIS_RULES.md` and generated rules text reference the same skill command surface.
* Tests cover command reference generation or static reference validation.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* rg -n "npm run polaris --" .polaris/skills src/workspace/.polaris/skills POLARIS_RULES.md src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* polaris-cli --help

## Ordering

* None. This is the first child and should complete before children that update graph guidance or adoption generation.

## Non-goals

* Do not change implementation-loop behavior.
* Do not execute worker dispatch.
* Do not remove compatibility aliases without documenting migration impact.

POL-381.title: ANALYZE: Canonical doctrine generation and genesis migration in Polaris adoption flow
